sourceMovie.title: Misery
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: People revisit Misery for the same reason it broke through in 1990: clear stakes, memorable faces, and a thriller rhythm that doesn't stall in the middle. Rob Reiner keeps the ensemble pointed in one direction. The drama and thriller blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: The Silence of the Lambs
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Misery clicked for you, The Silence of the Lambs (1991) is a strong next stop: both trade in drama and thriller, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Misery?
faq.answer: Try The Silence of the Lambs, 10 Cloverfield Lane, Crash—each shares DNA with Misery while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Misery?
faq.answer: Misery (1990) is best described as Drama, Thriller. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Misery worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 7.7/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this drama lane.
